question: Will Jinba Bowe tungsten export suspension impact on APT prices exceed 10 percent increase on May 31, 2026?
reason: Jinba Bowe's export suspension has significantly tightened global tungsten supply, leading to substantial price increases.
evidence: China's export volume of tungsten has declined by nearly 40% year-on-year since the implementation of export controls in February 2025. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/tungstenrises-to-record-highs-as-export-curbs-turn-up-supply-heat-4471841?utm_source=openai))

Ammonium paratungstate (APT) prices have surged from approximately $330 per metric ton unit (mtu) in December 2024 to around $2,050 per mtu by March 2026, reflecting a cumulative increase of over 520%. ([masanhightechmaterials.com](https://masanhightechmaterials.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/BCTC-TA-FINAL.pdf?utm_source=openai))

European APT prices have risen sharply, with reports indicating prices between $2,800 and $3,280 per mtu as of April 17, 2026, up 230.4% year-to-date. ([sct-usa.com](https://sct-usa.com/why-carbide-prices-are-rising?utm_source=openai))
